technique_name: trusted monotonic counter exploitation (state variable elimination)
claim: ob_authority_ts is always equal to ob_primary (both initialized to 1, both incremented by 1 in OBViewChange, never modified elsewhere), making ob_authority_ts a redundant state variable that can be eliminated by substituting ob_primary everywhere ob_authority_ts appears
properties_note: ob_authority_ts does not appear in any invariant or property (INV1-INV20). The only property that references authority_ts is INV6 (PhantomLeaderRejection) which uses ce_authority_ts, and INV17 (DownstreamCacheCoherence) which also uses only ce_authority_ts. OB authority_ts has zero property surface.

analysis.problem: ob_authority_ts is a per-market state variable (line 54) that occupies space in obVars, TypeInvariant, Init, and every UNCHANGED clause touching OB state. It tracks the authority timestamp for OB view changes. However, it is functionally identical to ob_primary: both are initialized to 1 (lines 169, 174), and both are incremented by exactly 1 in OBViewChange (lines 430-431, 437-438). No other action modifies either variable. They are always equal in every reachable state.
proof_of_equality: Init: ob_primary = [m in Markets |-> 1], ob_authority_ts = [m in Markets |-> 1]. Equal.
OBViewChange: new_ts = ob_authority_ts[m] + 1, new_session = ob_primary[m] + 1. Since ob_authority_ts[m] = ob_primary[m] by induction, new_ts = new_session.
All other actions: both appear in UNCHANGED. Neither is modified.
By induction on reachable states: ob_authority_ts[m] = ob_primary[m] for all m, always.
analysis.why_redundant: The OB view change message carries ts = new_ts (line 448). Downstream DownstreamOMSReceiveOBViewChange checks vc.ts > oms_cached_ob_ts[p][m] (line 609). Since new_ts = new_session always, the ts field in the VC message can carry new_session directly. The downstream monotonicity guard is preserved because ob_primary is strictly increasing (incremented by 1 on each view change, TEE crash-only prevents rollback).
analysis.why_not_ce: ce_authority_ts has the same equality with ce_primary, but ce_authority_ts appears in INV6 (PhantomLeaderRejection, line 893) and INV17 (DownstreamCacheCoherence, lines 971-972). Eliminating it would require property rewrites. ob_authority_ts has zero property surface, making it a clean elimination.
analysis.state_space_impact: Removes one per-market Nat variable from the state space. For TLC model checking, this directly reduces the state vector size by |Markets| * sizeof(Nat), improving exploration speed.

diff.description: Remove ob_authority_ts variable entirely. Replace its one read site (OBViewChange line 430) with ob_primary. Remove from VARIABLES, obVars, TypeInvariant, Init. Update all UNCHANGED clauses.
diff.unified_diff:
--- a/spec.tla
+++ b/spec.tla
@@ -51,9 +51,6 @@
     ob_pending_log,
     ob_alive,
 
-    \* --- OB authority timestamps (per-market) ---
-    ob_authority_ts,
-
     \* --- OMS state (per-partition) ---
     oms_seq,
@@ -97,7 +94,7 @@
 
 obVars == <<ob_seq, ob_log, ob_primary, ob_pending_seq, ob_pending_log,
-            ob_alive, ob_authority_ts>>
+            ob_alive>>
 
 omsVars == <<oms_seq, oms_log, oms_primary, oms_alive, oms_muted,
@@ -136,7 +133,6 @@
         /\ ob_alive[m] \in BOOLEAN
-        /\ ob_authority_ts[m] \in Nat
     /\ \A p \in Partitions :
@@ -173,7 +169,6 @@
     /\ ob_alive = [m \in Markets |-> TRUE]
-    /\ ob_authority_ts = [m \in Markets |-> 1]
     /\ oms_seq = [p \in Partitions |-> 1]
@@ -366,7 +361,7 @@
                    ce_checkpoint_seq, ce_last_recovered_seq, ce_backup_applied,
                    ce_backup_alive, ce_recovered_pending,
-                   ob_primary, ob_pending_seq, ob_pending_log, ob_alive, ob_authority_ts,
+                   ob_primary, ob_pending_seq, ob_pending_log, ob_alive,
                    msgs_ce_broadcast, msgs_ce_to_ob, msgs_view_change,
@@ -384,7 +379,7 @@
                    ce_checkpoint_seq, ce_last_recovered_seq, ce_backup_applied,
                    ce_backup_alive, ce_recovered_pending,
-                   ob_seq, ob_log, ob_primary, ob_alive, ob_authority_ts,
+                   ob_seq, ob_log, ob_primary, ob_alive,
                    msgs_ce_broadcast, msgs_ce_to_ob, msgs_ob_to_ce, msgs_view_change,
@@ -401,7 +396,7 @@
                    ce_checkpoint_seq, ce_last_recovered_seq, ce_backup_applied,
                    ce_backup_alive, ce_recovered_pending,
-                   ob_primary, ob_pending_seq, ob_pending_log, ob_alive, ob_authority_ts,
+                   ob_primary, ob_pending_seq, ob_pending_log, ob_alive,
                    msgs_ce_broadcast, msgs_ob_to_ce, msgs_view_change,
@@ -423,7 +418,7 @@
     /\ UNCHANGED <<ceVars, omsVars, cacheVars, msgVars, archiverVars, obBackupVars,
                    ob_seq, ob_log, ob_primary, ob_pending_seq, ob_pending_log,
-                   ob_authority_ts>>
+>>
 
 \* OB view change
 OBViewChange(m) ==
     /\ ~ob_alive[m]
-    /\ LET new_ts == ob_authority_ts[m] + 1
-           new_session == ob_primary[m] + 1
+    /\ LET new_session == ob_primary[m] + 1
            anchor == IF ob_backup_applied[m] /= {}
                      THEN MaxOfSet(ob_backup_applied[m])
                      ELSE 0
            available == OBArchiverSeqsForSession(m, ob_primary[m])
            recovered == MaxContiguousFrom(anchor + 1, available)
        IN /\ ob_primary' = [ob_primary EXCEPT ![m] = new_session]
-          /\ ob_authority_ts' = [ob_authority_ts EXCEPT ![m] = new_ts]
           /\ ob_log' = [ob_log EXCEPT ![m] = {e \in @ : e.seq <= recovered}]
           /\ ob_seq' = [ob_seq EXCEPT ![m] = recovered + 1]
           /\ ob_pending_seq' = [ob_pending_seq EXCEPT ![m] = 1]
           /\ ob_pending_log' = [ob_pending_log EXCEPT ![m] = {}]
           /\ ob_alive' = [ob_alive EXCEPT ![m] = TRUE]
           /\ ob_backup_applied' = [ob_backup_applied EXCEPT ![m] = {}]
           /\ msgs_view_change' = msgs_view_change \union
                  {[svc |-> "OB",
                    market |-> m,
-                   ts |-> new_ts,
+                   ts |-> new_session,
                    session |-> new_session,
                    last_recovered |-> recovered]}

changes.action: remove ob_authority_ts declaration and its comment block

changes.old:
obVars == <<ob_seq, ob_log, ob_primary, ob_pending_seq, ob_pending_log,
            ob_alive, ob_authority_ts>>
changes.new:
obVars == <<ob_seq, ob_log, ob_primary, ob_pending_seq, ob_pending_log,
            ob_alive>>

changes.old:
ob_seq, ob_log, ob_primary, ob_pending_seq, ob_pending_log,
                   ob_authority_ts>>

property_preservation.P1_TypeInvariant: ob_authority_ts removed from type invariant. All remaining fields unchanged. ob_primary[m] in Nat already guaranteed.
property_preservation.P6_PhantomLeaderRejection: Only references ce_authority_ts, not ob_authority_ts. Unaffected.
property_preservation.P10_OBLogBounded: References ob_log and ob_seq only. Unaffected.
property_preservation.P13_OBBackupAppliedValid: References ob_backup_applied and ob_log only. Unaffected.
property_preservation.P17_DownstreamCacheCoherence: References ob_cached_ce_ts and ce_authority_ts only. Does not reference ob_authority_ts. Unaffected.
property_preservation.downstream_monotonicity: vc.ts in the OB view change message changes from new_ts to new_session. Since new_ts = new_session always (proven by induction), DownstreamOMSReceiveOBViewChange guard (vc.ts > oms_cached_ob_ts) is preserved. The cached values start at 1 and track the same monotonic sequence.

verification_suggestion: Model check with Markets={m1,m2}, MaxSeq=4. Run original and modified specs. (1) All invariants INV1-INV20 should pass on both. (2) Verify state count decreases (fewer Nat values in state vector). (3) Trace: OB crashes twice for market m1. After first VC, ts=2,session=2. After second VC, ts=3,session=3. In modified spec, VC message carries ts=session in both cases. OMS monotonicity guard works identically.
risk_assessment: Minimal risk. Pure elimination of a provably redundant variable. No behavioral change in any reachable state. The inductive proof is trivial (same init, same increment, no other modifications). The only external effect is the VC message ts field, which carries the same numeric value by the equality invariant.
